data.title: United Internet AG reports 1H results
data.description:
• United Internet AG press release (OTCPK:UDIRF): 1H Sales of €3,099.9M (+2.8% Y/Y).
  • EBITDA amounted to €662.3M (prior year: €668.7M).
  • On August 2, 2024, United Internet adjusted its full-year guidance and now expects sales of approx. €6.4B in 2024 (previous guidance: approx. €6.5B; 2023: €6.213B). EBITDA is expected to amount to approx. €1.38B, including out-of-period expenses of -€14.3M, or approx. €1.39B without out-of-period expenses (previous guidance: approx. €1.42B; 2023: €1.30B).

More on United Internet AG

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, and Dow trade to the downside along with yields as they inch lower
data.description:
Major market averages are trade lower on Friday, a day after investors found some comfort from a bigger-than-expected decline in weekly initial jobless claims.

Early on and the S&P 500 (SP500) was -0.1%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.2%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) down 6 basis points to at 3.92%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 3 basis points to 4.01%.

Major U.S. stock indexes also ended higher on Thursday, as worries about the economy sliding into a recession were eased after the initial jobless claims came in less than expected.

"Barely a week since recessionary concerns rocked markets, investors have regained some poise, with share prices largely recouping the steep losses of the last few days," Richard Hunter, Interactive Investor said.

"Markets are closing out the week in a Disney paradise after having starting the week completely frozen! The buoyant market mood over the past 24 hours was given a significant boost by a decent U.S. jobless claims report," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

The better outturn pointed to enough strength in the U.S. labor market not to suggest that it is not falling apart. It certainly provided some evidence to the view that part of the weak payrolls may have been weather-related effects, Reid added.

Traders were focused on the initial jobless claims report, as they looked to gauge the labor market after last week's disappointing jobs report.

The economic calendar is light today, with only the Baker-Hughes rig count on tap.

data.title: Expensify raised to Market Outperform at JMP after mixed Q2 results; stock spikes
data.description:
JMP Securities upgraded Expensify (NASDAQ:EXFY) stock to Market Outperform from Market Perform on Friday after the expense management software platform turned in mixed Q2 results, with earnings topping the consensus and revenue falling short.

(EXFY) shot up 36% a day after posting its Q2 financials, trimming its year-to-date loss to 17%.

It was really the company's full-year outlook and earnings conference call that grabbed analyst Aaron Kimson's attention.

Expensify (EXFY) lifted its 2024 free cash flow guidance to $15.0M-$16.0M from $11.0M-$13.0M.

While Kimson still has concerns around competition, "this was the best earnings call that Expensify has had in two years," he wrote in a note to clients.

JMP's Market Outperform rate contrasts with the SA Quant system rating of Sell and aligns with the average Wall Street analyst rating of Buy.

data.title: Eventbrite stock suffers after Street analysts lower rating on weak forecast, results
data.description:
Multiple Wall Street research firms downgraded their rating on Eventbrite (NYSE:EB) following the company's mixed second quarter report and forecast that was short of analyst expectations, which sent its share down more than 30% on Friday. Analysts Truist, KeyBanc, and Piper Sandler weighed in with their commentary.

The company on Thursday after the bell said it expects full-year revenue of $318M to $325M, the midpoint of which is $321.5M, coming below Seeking Alpha's consensus estimate of $321.5M. It reported Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.01, beating the consensus estimate by six cents, but revenue of $84.55M missed expectations by $1.5M.

Shares fell as much as 34% to a 52-week low of $2.51 by early open-market New York trading. EB stock has lost over two-thirds of its value so far this year, while the benchmark S&P index is up 11.5%. EB is rated "sell" by SA's Quant ratings system and "hold" as per SA analysts on average.

Truist's takeaway: "We are downgrading EB to Hold following 2Q24 results, which were shy of already muted expectations, and more importantly, following 3Q & FY24 guidance that is well below consensus. This reflects stronger headwinds to paid ticket growth following mgmt's move to implement organizer fees. With visibility into the turnaround in ticket growth low (we now expect it in 2H25), pressure on the top line is likely to increase, keeping the stock range-bound NT. Mgmt also announced 11% RIF, which should help protect profitability and margins NT while it repositions itself for growth," the research firm said and withdrew its PT expectations vs. $11 previously assigned.

KeyBanc's takeaway: "We are downgrading shares of Eventbrite from Overweight to Sector Weight as our thesis on monetization and margin inflection has changed. Eventbrite acknowledged it pushed too hard with its monetization strategy and is shifting back to having a free tier to recapture lost creators. While this is an appropriate action to reorient the platform, this materially changes 2H24E financials and creates uncertainty on how quickly creators will return to the platform. We assume this is a multiquarter issue and now expect ~3% y/y revenue growth in 2025E," the research firm said. It had no PT assigned to the stock.

Piper Sandler's takeaway: "We are downgrading Eventbrite to Neutral following another estimate reset and a strategy pivot. The company lowered its 2024 revenue growth outlook to essentially flat year-over-year on the back of further paid ticket volume deterioration. Management has made the decision to backtrack on its capacity-based organizer fees in order to bring creators back to the platform. While we applaud the pivot, the return to paid ticket volume was pushed out even further and will likely get worse before it gets better. In order to adjust to the lower growth outlook, management has decided to reduce headcount by about 11%. Overall, there are growth levers on the horizon, but now we need to see sustained (multi-quarter) momentum in paid ticket volume before getting more constructive," the research firm said and lowered its PT to $4 from $9, implying a 47% upside to share price at current levels.
